messages.content:
I am getting an 'Object Required' error with this code:  Sub OutstandingPO()

'Application.EnableEvents = False

'Declare variables
Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long, j As Long
Dim foundRows As Collection, cell As Range
Dim txtFile As Integer, txtFilePath As String, txtContent As String

Worksheets("PORDER").Activate

'Find last row in column A
lastRow = ActiveSheet.Cells(ActiveSheet.Rows.count, 1).End(xlUp).Row

'Create a collection to store found rows
Set foundRows = New Collection

'Loop through column A from last row upwards
For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
If Not IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1)) Then 'If the cell has a formula value
'Loop through column G for empty cells in the same row
For j = 1 To 6 'Columns A to F
If IsEmpty(Cells(i, j + 6)) Then 'If the cell is empty
'Add the row to the collection
foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value
Exit For 'Exit the inner loop
End If
Next j
End If
Next i

'If no matches were found, exit the sub
If foundRows.count = 0 Then
MsgBox "No matches found.", vbInformation
Exit Sub
End If

'Open Notepad and create a new file
txtFilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\zzzz ServProvDocs\AwaitingPO.txt"
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile

'Loop through the collection and store the data as a string
For Each cell In foundRows
txtContent = txtContent & Join(cell, vbTab) & vbCrLf
Next cell

'Write the string to the file and close it
Print #txtFile, txtContent
Close txtFile

'Message to confirm successful export
MsgBox "Total matches found: " & foundRows.count & vbCrLf & _
"Data exported to " & txtFilePath, vbInformation

'Application.EnableEvents = True

End Sub

The problem might be with the line "foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value", where you are trying to add a range object to the collection instead of its value. Try changing it to "foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value", and see if that resolves the error.
after applying your changes, I now get the error File is Already Open

This error is occurring because the file "AwaitingPO.txt" is already open. Before opening the file for output, you can check if it is already open and close it if necessary.

Try adding the following code to the beginning of your sub:

```
'Remove file lock if file already open
On Error Resume Next
Dim fileNumber As Integer
fileNumber = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Binary Access Read Write Lock Read Write As #fileNumber
Close #fileNumber
On Error GoTo 0
```

This checks if the file is already open and unlocks it if necessary before opening it for output. This should prevent the "File is Already Open" error.
After adding the above code it is still giving me the same error

If the previous solution did not work, you can try closing the file before opening it for output. You can modify the code for opening the file as follows:

```
'Close file if already open
On Error Resume Next
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    Close #txtFile
    txtFile = FreeFile()
    Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
End If
On Error GoTo 0
```

This will first try to open the file for output. If it is already open, it will close it and then open it again for output. This should remove any locks on the file and allow your code to write to it.

I hope this helps!

I am now getting the error 'Object Required'. I also include the entire code with the changes we have made: Sub OutstandingPO()

'Application.EnableEvents = False

'Declare variables
Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long, j As Long
Dim foundRows As Collection, cell As Range
Dim txtFile As Integer, txtFilePath As String, txtContent As String

Worksheets("PORDER").Activate

'Find last row in column A
lastRow = ActiveSheet.Cells(ActiveSheet.Rows.count, 1).End(xlUp).Row

'Create a collection to store found rows
Set foundRows = New Collection

'Loop through column A from last row upwards
For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
If Not IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1)) Then 'If the cell has a formula value
'Loop through column G for empty cells in the same row
For j = 1 To 6 'Columns A to F
If IsEmpty(Cells(i, j + 6)) Then 'If the cell is empty
'Add the row to the collection
foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value
Exit For 'Exit the inner loop
End If
Next j
End If
Next i

'If no matches were found, exit the sub
If foundRows.count = 0 Then
MsgBox "No matches found.", vbInformation
Exit Sub
End If

'Remove file lock if file already open
On Error Resume Next
Dim fileNumber As Integer
fileNumber = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Binary Access Read Write Lock Read Write As #fileNumber
Close #fileNumber
On Error GoTo 0

'Open Notepad and create a new file
txtFilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\zzzz ServProvDocs\AwaitingPO.txt"
txtFile = FreeFile()
'Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile

'Close file if already open
On Error Resume Next
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
Close #txtFile
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
End If
On Error GoTo 0

'Loop through the collection and store the data as a string
For Each cell In foundRows
txtContent = txtContent & Join(cell, vbTab) & vbCrLf
Next cell

'Write the string to the file and close it
Print #txtFile, txtContent
Close txtFile

'Message to confirm successful export
MsgBox "Total matches found: " & foundRows.count & vbCrLf & _
"Data exported to " & txtFilePath, vbInformation

'Application.EnableEvents = True

End Sub

I see the issue now. In the line where you open the file for output, the variable `txtFilePath` has not yet been assigned a value, so it is empty. Therefore, you are trying to open a non-existent file.

You need to move the line where you define `txtFilePath` to before the code block where you check if the file is already open. Try this modification:

```
'Open Notepad and create a new file
txtFilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\zzzz ServProvDocs\AwaitingPO.txt"

'Remove file lock if file already open
On Error Resume Next
Dim fileNumber As Integer
fileNumber = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Binary Access Read Write Lock Read Write As #fileNumber
Close #fileNumber
On Error GoTo 0

txtFile = FreeFile()
'Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile

'Close file if already open
On Error Resume Next
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
Close #txtFile
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
End If
On Error GoTo 0

'Loop through the collection and store the data as a string
For Each cell In foundRows
txtContent = txtContent & Join(cell, vbTab) & vbCrLf
Next cell

'Write the string to the file and close it
Print #txtFile, txtContent
Close txtFile

'Message to confirm successful export
MsgBox "Total matches found: " & foundRows.count & vbCrLf & _
"Data exported to " & txtFilePath, vbInformation
```

This should solve the 'Object Required' error.

'Application.EnableEvents = False

'Declare variables

Exit Sub

txtFile = FreeFile()
'Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile

Close #txtFile

End If
On Error GoTo 0

'Loop through the collection and store the data as a string

txtContent = txtContent & Join(cell, vbTab) & vbCrLf
Next cell

'Write the string to the file and close it
Print #txtFile, txtContent
Close txtFile

'Message to confirm successful export
MsgBox "Total matches found: " & foundRows.count & vbCrLf & _
"Data exported to " & txtFilePath, vbInformation

'Application.EnableEvents = True

End Sub

I see the issue now. The problem is with the line "foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value", which should be "foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6).Value)". You are missing a closing parenthesis in this line which is causing the error.

This is the corrected code:

```
'Loop through column A from last row upwards
For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
If Not IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1)) Then 'If the cell has a formula value
'Loop through column G for empty cells in the same row
For j = 1 To 6 'Columns A to F
If IsEmpty(Cells(i, j + 6)) Then 'If the cell is empty
'Add the row to the collection
foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value
Exit For 'Exit the inner loop
End If
Next j
End If
Next i
```

This should resolve the 'Object Required' error.
still getting the object required error

I apologize for the confusion. I see another issue with the code. In the line "If IsEmpty(Cells(i, j + 6)) Then", you need to add "ActiveSheet." before Cells to make sure it is referencing the correct sheet.

Here is the corrected version of that line:

```
If IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, j + 6)) Then
```

I hope this helps resolve the 'Object Required' error.
Still getting the Object Required error

I apologize for the ongoing issues with this code. The issue may be with the line "foundRows.Add Range(Cells(i, 1), Cells(i, 6)).Value". This line is trying to add a range object to the collection, but it should be adding an array of values instead.

Please try changing that line to the following:

```
foundRows.Add Array(Cells(i, 1).Value, Cells(i, 2).Value, Cells(i, 3).Value, Cells(i, 4).Value, Cells(i, 5).Value, Cells(i, 6).Value)
```

This creates an array of values for each row and adds that array to the collection.

I hope this helps resolve the 'Object Required' error.

Still getting the 'Object Required' error from this code:  Sub OutstandingPO()

'Application.EnableEvents = False

'Declare variables
Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long, j As Long
Dim foundRows As Collection, cell As Range
Dim txtFile As Integer, txtFilePath As String, txtContent As String

Worksheets("PORDER").Activate

'Find last row in column A
lastRow = ActiveSheet.Cells(ActiveSheet.Rows.count, 1).End(xlUp).Row

'Create a collection to store found rows
Set foundRows = New Collection

'Loop through column A from last row upwards
For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
If Not IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1)) Then 'If the cell has a formula value
'Loop through column G for empty cells in the same row
For j = 1 To 6 'Columns A to F
If IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, j + 6)) Then 'If the cell is empty
'Add the row to the collection
foundRows.Add Array(Cells(i, 1).Value, Cells(i, 2).Value, Cells(i, 3).Value, Cells(i, 4).Value, Cells(i, 5).Value, Cells(i, 6).Value)
Exit For 'Exit the inner loop
End If
Next j
End If
Next i

'If no matches were found, exit the sub
If foundRows.count = 0 Then
MsgBox "No matches found.", vbInformation
Exit Sub
End If

'Open Notepad and create a new file
txtFilePath = "G:\Shared drives\Swan School Site Premises\PREMISES MANAGEMENT\SERVICE PROVIDERS\zzzz ServProvDocs\AwaitingPO.txt"

'Remove file lock if file already open
On Error Resume Next
Dim fileNumber As Integer
fileNumber = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Binary Access Read Write Lock Read Write As #fileNumber
Close #fileNumber
On Error GoTo 0

txtFile = FreeFile()
'Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile

'Close file if already open
On Error Resume Next
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
Close #txtFile
txtFile = FreeFile()
Open txtFilePath For Output Lock Write As #txtFile
End If
On Error GoTo 0

'Loop through the collection and store the data as a string
For Each cell In foundRows
txtContent = txtContent & Join(cell, vbTab) & vbCrLf
Next cell

'Write the string to the file and close it
Print #txtFile, txtContent
Close txtFile

'Message to confirm successful export
MsgBox "Total matches found: " & foundRows.count & vbCrLf & _
"Data exported to " & txtFilePath, vbInformation

'Application.EnableEvents = True

End Sub

I apologize for the issue. The last possibility I can think of is that you need to add "ActiveSheet." before "Cells" in the line:

```
foundRows.Add Array(Cells(i, 1).Value, Cells(i, 2).Value, Cells(i, 3).Value, Cells(i, 4).Value, Cells(i, 5).Value, Cells(i, 6).Value)
```

it should be updated to be:

```
foundRows.Add Array(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1).Value, ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 2).Value, ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 3).Value, ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 4).Value, ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 5).Value, ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 6).Value)
```

This ensures that the cells being referenced are on the active sheet. I hope that this should resolve the 'Object Required' error.

I apologize for the continued issue. One other possibility is that the reference to column G in the header comment might be causing the issue. Column G is not being used in the code, only columns A to F. Can you try replacing the comment "'Loop through column G for empty cells in the same row" with "'Loop through columns A to F for empty cells in the same row" to make sure there is no conflict? 

Here is the updated code with the comments modified:

```
'Application.EnableEvents = False

'Declare variables
Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long, j As Long
Dim foundRows As Collection, cell As Range
Dim txtFile As Integer, txtFilePath As String, txtContent As String

Worksheets("PORDER").Activate

'Find last row in column A
lastRow = ActiveSheet.Cells(ActiveSheet.Rows.count, 1).End(xlUp).Row

'Create a collection to store found rows
Set foundRows = New Collection

'Loop through columns A to F from last row upwards
For i = lastRow To 1 Step -1
If Not IsEmpty(ActiveSheet.Cells(i, 1)) Then 'If the cell has a formula value
'Loop through columns
